page: Amazon Web Services g5.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz and includes 16 MB of L3 cache. System memory consists of 32 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, translating to 4 GB of memory per core.

The instance is equipped with a single NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU featuring 22 GB of dedicated VRAM, making it tailored for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Local storage is provided via a 450 GB NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance type.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance trade-off. While the CPU performance is relatively modest, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core benchmarks scoring in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively, the GPU-accelerated capabilities are highly competitive. LLM inference benchmarks for both small (135M) and medium (7B) models place this instance in the top 10% for prompt processing and text generation. General compute tasks, such as Geekbench compilation, image processing, and Redis SET operations, perform in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g5.2xlarge offers a balanced entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads, trading off raw CPU performance for high-density graphics and AI inference capabilities. It is highly suited for workloads such as low-to-medium parameter LLM inference, real-time ray tracing, image processing, and graphics-heavy applications. It is less optimal for CPU-bound high-performance computing or memory-intensive database operations that do not leverage the GPU.
description: Amazon Web Services g5.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features an AMD EPYC 7R32 CPU with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 450 GB local NVMe SSD. The hardware profile is highlighted by a single NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. While synthetic CPU benchmarks show modest performance, with stress-ng scores falling in the lower tiers, the instance excels in GPU-driven tasks. Notably, LLM inference benchmarks for 135M and 7B models rank in the top 10% for both prompt processing and text generation. This instance is highly suited for machine learning inference, ray tracing, and image processing workloads, offering a cost-effective balance of GPU acceleration and local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an AMD EPYC CPU, 32 GB RAM, a 450 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB VRAM, optimized for machine learning inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5.2xlarge features an AMD EPYC CPU, 32 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA A10G GPU, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance combining dedicated AMD EPYC processing, local NVMe storage, and NVIDIA Ampere graphics for efficient machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Equipped with one NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB VRAM
Features 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32 GB DDR4 RAM
Includes 450 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers top-tier LLM inference speeds for 135M and 7B models
Best suited for machine learning inference, ray tracing, and graphics tasks
